characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: A super-powered, sex-obsessed lothario whose machismo hides a fragile ego.Initially a minor character, but eventually came to dominate the show.ParodiesSupermanas well as the macho jock stereotype. Alternate Company Equivalent: ACorrupted Character Copyof Superman with a depraved, violent, and selfish, immature streak a mile long? Captain Hero might as well beComedy Central's version ofHomelander, or more appropriatelyhis clone, Black Noir. Ambiguously Jewish: A subtleShout-Outto Superman, who sometimes gets a similar treatment due to his creators being Jewish. Ascended Extra: Despite his introduction into the series as simply "another person in the house" and his subsequent relatively minor role in the first season of the show, the episode "Little Orphan Hero" in the following season acted as aWham Episodefor the character, in turn opening up his depravity and deep-seated emotional and mental issues to reveal a far more complex and malleable character than earlier episodes had implied. Subsequently, a rapid onslaught of being beloved by the creators ensued, leading Captain Hero's prominence within the show to rise to the extent where, by the series's conclusion, he had virtually (albeit unofficially) assumed the role of the show's main character. Ax-Crazy: He will murder anyone who gets in his way.

characters.name: Captain Hero
characters.description: A super-powered, sex-obsessed lothario whose machismo hides a fragile ego.Initially a minor character, but eventually came to dominate the show.ParodiesSupermanas well as the macho jock stereotype. Alternate Company Equivalent: ACorrupted Character Copyof Superman with a depraved, violent, and selfish, immature streak a mile long? Captain Hero might as well beComedy Central's version ofHomelander, or more appropriatelyhis clone, Black Noir. Ambiguously Jewish: A subtleShout-Outto Superman, who sometimes gets a similar treatment due to his creators being Jewish. Ascended Extra: Despite his introduction into the series as simply "another person in the house" and his subsequent relatively minor role in the first season of the show, the episode "Little Orphan Hero" in the following season acted as aWham Episodefor the character, in turn opening up his depravity and deep-seated emotional and mental issues to reveal a far more complex and malleable character than earlier episodes had implied. Subsequently, a rapid onslaught of being beloved by the creators ensued, leading Captain Hero's prominence within the show to rise to the extent where, by the series's conclusion, he had virtually (albeit unofficially) assumed the role of the show's main character. Ax-Crazy: He will murder anyone who gets in his way.

characters.name: Foxxy Love
characters.description: A1970s-style mystery-solving musician who parodies theHanna-Barberateen sleuth characters seen onJosie And The PussycatsandScooby-Doo. She plays the tokenSassy Black Woman/put-upon minority (alsothe Village Bicycle, though most of the cast competes for that title). Foxxy was the show's most heavily featured character until Captain Hero took over midway through Season Two. Accidental Misnaming: She's quite bad at remembering names, especially Toot's, whose name she gets wrong no less than three times in a single episode. In "Trapped in Parking Space Pt. 1", she even confuses her own name with the name hers references, Foxy Brown. Berserk Button: "Now if I write my name on a bag of Funyuns, them ain'tyoFunyuns! Them'sFoxxy'sFunyuns!" Black Jezebel Stereotype: Foxxy Love isthe only black womanamong the main cast. She's also theMs. Fanserviceof the group and has avery strong sexual appetite even compared to her housemates, resulting in numerous abortions and abandoned children. She's often contrasted against the white Princess Clara, who is not only more innocent but very racist; in the first episode, the two girls make out, with Clara literally singing the praises of the "black chick's tongue." Her name is also an explicit reference to Foxy Brown. Book Dumb: She's absolutely terrible at math as a reference to her stereotype.

characters.name: Ling-Ling
characters.description: An ill-tempered anime character (specifically, a parody ofPikachu). He speaks infaux-Japanese babble (his voice actress calls it "Japorean"...)and seldom interacts much with the other characters (who usually don't understand a word he says). He plays the fish-out-of-water foreigner/Team Pet. He is also incredibly violent (expect bucketloads of blood in any of his scenes), which parodies the tendency for Japanese animation to be far more violent than its Western counterparts despite both being aimed at a younger audience. All Anime Is Naughty Tentacles: Not with actual tentacles, but he does play into the stereotype that "all anime is violence and sex". Ambiguous Gender: He was previously thought to have an unknown gender, and he did things that implied he wasswitching back and forth between male and femaledepending on the scene he was in. It is eventually confirmed in "Clum Babies" that he is male. Arranged Marriage: He has an "Arranged Battle" with Ni-Pul, a busty purpleCat Girlmonster with a rocket launcher tail, in the episode "Clum Babies". As Long as It Sounds Foreign: The dialogue his subtitles translate isn't a real Asian language and is just random gibberish.

characters.name: Princess Clara
characters.description: A parody of both the genericDisney Princess(to the point of her design being very close toAriel's) and the naive airhead, complete with the ability tospontaneously generate fuzzy woodland creatures when she sings. Anobsessive religious fanatic, she also parodies the "upper-class WASP" stereotype by being condescendingly biased against some ethnic and religious groups. Attention Whore: Becomes so addicted to the praise she gets by being Wooldoor's "caregiver" when he becomes ill, shepoisons him with drain cleaner to keep him sick, just so she can keep basking in the kudos. Belief Makes You Stupid: The example the show cuts to when demonstrating her gullibility is her converting to Christianity after only just learning about the concept from Wooldoor. Beware the Nice Ones: At times. Multiple episodes demonstrate her as being adept at handling firearms, and in one episode, she hacks the proprietor of a torture enterprise to death with a sword.Rhino Guard:Do you not know that impersonating a princess is a serious offense, punishable by death?(Clara slaps him.)Clara:Do you not know that sucking mydickis a serious offense, punishable byfuck you?! Now let us in at once or I'll have your children killed! Butt-Monkey: She tends to be the most physically abused of the characters.

characters.name: Spanky Ham
characters.description: An anthropomorphic pig based onInternet cartoonswho enjoys exercising his warped, disgusting sense of humor. Parodies Internet Flash animations, adult animation (both on- and offline) that relies on disgusting, often sexual humor (and use Flash animation), and the "party animal" stereotype. Ambiguously Jewish: Despite being a pig, a Muslim, and a Scientologist. Animated Shock Comedy: ThoughDrawn Togetheras a whole is one, he's specifically based on this kind of animation. Big Ol' Unibrow: Spanky's voice actor Adam Carolla thinks it's a reference to his own. The Bully: Uses and abuses thehellout of poor Wooldoor Sockbat throughout almost all of "Requiem for a Reality Show". Xandir is also a frequent target.

characters.name: Toot Braunstein
characters.description: A black-and-white cartoon gal from the1920swho's an overweight, bitter, washed-up, borderline-psychotic, alcoholic parody ofBetty Boop. She fills the role of the "bitch" character after her attempts at being the sexpot failed. Abhorrent Admirer: She's attracted to Xandir and is so clingy and repulsive that Xandir would likely still want nothing to do with herif he wasn't gay. Abusive Parents: Her mom was probably more abusive than her dad since in "Mexican't Buy Me Love" she tells her to "suck on it". The Alcoholic: One of the heaviest drinkers in the cast. Ambiguously Jewish: Considering her surname ends with "stein" and that she's a parody of Betty Boop.

characters.name: Wooldoor Sockbat
characters.description: A goofy yellow whatchamacallit who parodies contemporary kids' cartoon characters. Inspirations for this character includeSpongeBob SquarePants, Stimpy fromThe Ren & Stimpy Show, any of the Warner brothers (and sister) fromAnimaniacs, any ofTex Avery's wacky characterswhen Avery worked for MGM, and basically whatever the plot demands. He plays the attention-seekingTeam Pet. Ambiguous Innocence: On one hand, he's a cheerful and happy-go-luckyKeet. On the other hand, he has done some heinous actions throughout the show. Ambiguously Jewish: In a few episodes, he goes to synagogue, once wore a yamulke, and mentioned celebrating Hannukah. However, he's stated he's a Christian (in "Clum Babies"). Attention Deficit... Ooh, Shiny!: He does not pay attention to anything. Break the Cutie: Despite how he's meant to be the most innocent of the housemates, he oftentimes gets the short end of the stick.

characters.name: Xandir P. Wifflebottom
characters.description: An effeminate (later openly gay) elf swordsman and video game character who parodies Link from theThe Legend of Zeldagames (andVideo Gamesin general). His role on the show is that of theCamp Gay(after beingAmbiguously Gayfor the first couple of episodes). All Gays are Promiscuous: He comes out of the closet in episode 3. By the very next episode, he has already lost his gag reflex. Bare Midriffs Are Feminine: He wears an outfit that exposes his stomach, to emphasize his effeminate,Camp Gaypersonality. Boomerang Bigot: Downplayed. He isn't nearly as offensive regarding Ling-Ling's ethnicity with the others, but still idly contributes to it. This despite being based on Link from the Legend of Zelda, which is also a Japanese character with an anime-esque design. In the logic of the Drawn Together universe Xandir is also Japanese but never so much as mentions it to the cast, nor exhibits any stereotypical behavior. Camp Gay: He acts effeminate and very frequently expresses his fondness for sex.

characters.name: The Jew Producer
characters.description: The only non-housemate to have any sort of recurring importance to the show. Originally, "Jew Producer" was just his nickname amongst the cast until he established that "The Jew Producer" was, in fact,his name. Author Avatar: He is the in-show representation of the show's creators, a rare villainous example of the trope. Aw, Look! They Really Do Love Each Other: The movie shows that deep down, he truly does care about the housemates. Bad Boss: Never misses an opportunity to abuse his power. Big Bad: The closest this series has to one, given that he created the show and orders everyone around.

characters.name: Judge Fudge
characters.description: Adaptational Badass: In "The Judge Fudge Power Hour", Judge Fudge's life as a crime-stopping badass is delved into. The Cameo: He makes one inThe Movieon a transition screen telling the viewers to put on their 3D glasses when the housemates arrive to Make-a-Point Land. Catchphrase: "I'm far too busy being delicious."

characters.name: Octopussoir
characters.description: A tentacle monster that lives in Clara's vagina. Eldritch Abomination: What could be more horrifying than a tentacle monster, coming out of one's pussy? Naughty Tentacles: He is literally a bunch of octopus-like tentacles that live inside a woman's vagina. Non-Malicious Monster: Despite his horrific appearance, he is actually a super nice guy, who donates to charity, does people's chores, and helps his neighbors. Put on a Bus: In "Alzheimer's Ends Well", he was surgically removed from Princess Clara and was sent off to live a life of his own.

characters.name: Bleh
characters.description: Beautiful All Along: At first it looks like she's grotesque beneath her helmet and clothes, but then she takes off her helmet,drops her bathrobeand beginsShaking Her Hair Looseand shows she's just as attractive as Clara. Fish Eyes: Her eyes face two different directions or they cross at different points. Hair Color Dissonance: In her debut appearance, her hair's red (or a reddish brown), while in "Ghostesses in the Slot Machine", her hair is brown. It's not clear if that's intentional or an animation error. Hidden Depths: Tying intoDisabled Means Helpless, she's actually smarter than she lets on, as she made a bet with Spanky's equally impaired cousin that she would date Captain Hero for $50 (in "The Other Cousin"). Likewise, she's shown to be able to get out of her crib when she was put in it and she's also as sex-crazed as (if not more than) the rest of the cast. Inspirationally Disadvantaged: Type B or, more likely, a parody of it.

characters.name: Strawberry Sweetcake
characters.description: Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: Her innocent nature hides the fact that she's actually a genocidal dictator. Casting Gag: Her voice actress, Cree Summer, previously worked on theStrawberry Shortcake (1980s)animated specials as the voice of Orange Blossom. Deliberately Cute Child: While technically eighteen, she still looks and acts like an eight-year-old to keep people too distracted by her cuteness to notice her cruelty. Eaten Alive:She gets devoured byTootafter the latter crashes into the house and spots Strawberry Sweetcake amidst a pile of Sockbat chocolates. The Fake Cutie: At first, the housemates are overwhelmed by her cuteness, but then it's revealed that she's anything but sweet.

characters.name: Mickey Mouse
characters.description: *Bleep*-dammit!: Combined withClumsy Copyright Censorship, when his name is spoken aloud by Clara, his first name is censored. When it's said again by Hero, his last name is censored. Culture Police: His plan is to irradicate all politically incorrect cartoons rather than be forced to acknowledge animation's uncomfortable history. Lawyer-Friendly Cameo: In a series that's already filled with such, but in Mickey's case, parts of his name are always censored whenever said name is spoken. Politically Correct Villain: He is the antagonist in "Terms Of Endearment", and wants to eradicate every politically incorrect cartoon caricature and racial stereotype. Shadowed Face, Glowing Eyes: Is completely silhouetted save for his eyes.

characters.name: The Veggie Fables
characters.description: Anthropomorphic Food: Just like the characters they're parodying, they are vegetables who talk and sing. Ax-Crazy: Bob, whoends up killing nearly the entire cast at the end of the episode. Dark Parody: They're theVeggieTalescast if instead of being an optimistic and kid-friendly program to teach children about the Bible, they instead just used scare tactics, guilt trips, and, inBob's case, outright murderto get their point across. The Bob and Larry expies are so blatant that the only difference is their names are swapped. Depraved Kids' Show Host: They run a children's Christian cartoon according to Wooldoor but use extreme scare tactics that would definitely frighten any child in order to spread their message, with their song threatening Wooldor with Hell if he doesn't repent. One of them is even clinically psychotic, andpromptly murders everyone as soon as he doesn’t get his way. Driven to Suicide:Bob kills himself after a Clum Baby cures his mental instability, allowing him to realize the horror of his mass murder.

characters.name: The Board of Education
characters.description: Evil Plan: Dissatisfied with education not being that profitable, the Board of Education came up with a racist scheme of making money by selling tacky merchandise to black people and deliberately making them fail theirSATs to keep them uneducated enough to waste their money on his crap. Expy: He's a parody of the Bill from theSchoolhouse Rock!song "I'm Just a Bill". Mortarboard of Intelligence: He dons a mortarboard when he tells Foxxy of the history of education in the United States. No Celebrities Were Harmed: As apropos of aSchoolhouse Rockparody, his voice is an imitation of Jack Sheldon. Politically Incorrect Villain: He describes his plan as combining two methods of achieving wealth: selling stupid stuff to suckers and exploiting the black man.

characters.name: Phat Allen and his Junkyard Pals
characters.description: Captain Ersatz: They are clear stand-ins forFat Albert and the Cosby Kids. Lawyer-Friendly Cameo: When first introduced, the Dumb Donald expy goes out of his way to emphasize that Allen hasabsolutely no relationto Fat Albert. Lyrical Dissonance: They sing a catchy song about how implanted memories are good because they give you an excuse to deny responsibility for your actions and be an all-around horrible person.Na na, you can be a total assholeNa na, you can be a total shitNa na, be a child-molesting, serial killer cannibalNa na, tell the world to suck your fucking dick! Not a Mask: Not Bright Donald, the Dumb Donald expy, ends up having his hat torn off to reveal skinless musculature. The Scapegoat: Wooldoor brainwashes Foxxy into believing Phat Allen and his pals sexually assaulted her as a little girl so she would feel better about being a total slut. He doesn't reveal the truth until she's murdered all of them except Phat Allen in revenge.

characters.name: Bizarro Captain Hero
characters.description: Beard of Evil: He has a beard unlike the clean-shaven Captain Hero. Bury Your Gays: It's strongly implied that he had a gay affair with Captain Hero. Captain Hero ends up hanging him to keep him quiet. We Hardly Knew Ye: Is killed off in the same episode he first appears in.

characters.name: Sid
characters.description: Black Comedy: His entire existence is a rather morbid pun on Sudden Infant Death Syndrome (SIDS), as he's introduced as a new member of the cast in "Drawn Together Babies", only to unceremoniously die as soon as the opening credits are over. Death of a Child: He's a baby who ends up dying from sleeping on his stomach. Punny Name: His name is a play on SIDS (Sudden Infant Death Syndrome) and he ends up dying from sleeping on his stomach, a common cause of SIDS. We Hardly Knew Ye: He dies not long after being introduced.

characters.name: Scroto
characters.description: Bald of Evil: He's a bald enemy of Captain Hero. Captain Ersatz: He is based on Superman's enemy Lex Luthor. Evil Is Petty: He is willing to pretend he's dying as a long-term scheme to trick Captain Hero into washing his testicles for him. Captain Hero reveals that this is the only evil act he ever does - no robberies or global domination schemes; he just keeps tricking Captain Hero over and over and over into washing his privates. Wounded Gazelle Gambit: He throws himself under Captain Hero's speeding car to injure himself, catch his nemesis off guard, and launch his "scheme" into motion.

characters.name: Munchkin Mouse
characters.description: The cast member who gets voted off in the series finale. Alliterative Name:MunchkinMouse Deus ex Machina: He exists solely to keep any of the actual cast members from being voted off the show. Remember the New Guy?: Parodied. He only ever appears in the series finale to get voted off, and the montage showcasing his previous appearances all involve him being crudely pasted into previous scenes.

characters.name: The Boss
characters.description: The head of the network which airedDrawn TogetherIn-Universe. Only appeared inThe Movieas the main antagonist. Bad Boss: He tries to force the Jew Producer to kill the Drawn Together cast against his will. The Bad Guy Wins: Posthumously. Although he dies, his last Eraser Bomb wipes out the surviving Drawn Together cast. Big Bad: He is the main antagonist ofThe Movie. Facial Horror: Half of his face is horribly scarred.

characters.name: I.S.R.A.E.L.
characters.description: The Boss's robotic henchwoman whose job it is to permanently erase the housemates. Badass Israeli: Ruthless in a fight and personifies the state of Israel. Deader than Dead: Her bombs can permanently erase cartoon characters from existence. She gets erased with the housemates at the end of the film thanks to Spanky accidentally stepping on one. Does This Remind You of Anything?: Much of her dialogue is thinly veiled references to the state of Israel. The Dragon: She kills cartoon characters on orders from the Boss.

characters.name: Suck My Taint Girl
characters.description: The central character ofThe Suck My Taint Show. Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: She acts like a nice little girl and even acts helpful towards theDrawn Togethercast when she hears their show is getting canceled, but in reality she's crude, mean, disgusting, and manipulative. Even Evil Has Standards: She becomes visibly horrified and disgusted by the housemates' disgusting antics. Expy: OfCartman, as one might expect, with "Suck my taint" line being a parody of Cartman's "You can suck my balls" and her being drawn and animated in a similar way. Older Than She Looks: She looks and acts 9 but she's alsothe Network Head's wifeand is seen smoking.
